summary: Reporter passes a .lottie file (dotLottie ZIP container format) to SkiaSharp.Skottie.Animation.Create(path) and gets null back. The Skottie API only parses Lottie JSON, not the compressed dotLottie (.lottie) ZIP container, so the data fails to parse silently.

stepsToReproduce: Download a .lottie file from LottieFiles (dotLottie format, which is a ZIP container)
Observe that the returned Animation is null

screenshots.description: Debug view showing null animation result

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The Skottie API has not changed to add dotLottie support since 3.116.0. The underlying limitation is in Skia's Skottie module which only parses JSON.

analysis.summary: The .lottie (dotLottie) format is a ZIP container that wraps a Lottie JSON file plus assets. Skia's Skottie module only accepts raw Lottie JSON data. When a .lottie ZIP file is passed to Animation.Create(path), the raw ZIP bytes are fed to skottie::Animation::Make() which expects JSON, fails to parse, and returns nullptr — translated to null in C#. This is not a bug but an unsupported format. The workaround is to extract the JSON from the ZIP before passing it to Skottie.
analysis.rationale: Classified as enhancement rather than bug because the Skottie API works correctly for its designed input (Lottie JSON). The .lottie format is a separate container specification that would require decompression logic to support. The reporter likely did not realize .lottie and .json are different formats. Not a feature-request because Skottie support already exists — this would improve existing functionality to handle the compressed variant.

keySignals.interpretation: The .lottie extension indicates dotLottie format (ZIP container), not standard Lottie JSON
LottieFiles offers both .json and .lottie downloads. The reporter downloaded the .lottie (ZIP) format.

keySignals.text: No exception is thrown, so it fails silently
keySignals.interpretation: This is expected factory-returns-null pattern when Skia cannot parse the data.

codeInvestigation.finding: Animation.Create(string path) reads the file via SKData.Create(path) and passes raw bytes to skottie_animation_make_from_data(). No format detection or decompression is performed — the raw bytes (ZIP header for .lottie files) are sent directly to Skia's JSON parser.
skottie_animation_make_from_data() calls skottie::Animation::Make(data, length) which expects JSON text data. ZIP binary data will fail to parse and return nullptr.
GetPreambleSize() only handles BOM preambles (UTF-8 BOM, UTF-16 BOM, UTF-7, UTF-32 BOM). It does not detect or handle ZIP file signatures (PK header 0x50 0x4B).

workarounds: Extract the Lottie JSON from the .lottie ZIP container first (e.g., using System.IO.Compression.ZipArchive), then pass the JSON string to Animation.Parse() or the JSON stream to Animation.Create(stream).
Download the .json format from LottieFiles instead of the .lottie format.
nextQuestions: Should SkiaSharp add built-in dotLottie decompression, or is this the responsibility of the caller?
Does upstream Skia have any plans to support dotLottie natively?

resolution.hypothesis: The .lottie file is a ZIP container, not raw JSON. Skia's Skottie parser only accepts JSON input, so ZIP data returns null.

proposals.title: Use .json format instead of .lottie
proposals.description: Download the Lottie animation in .json format from LottieFiles instead of .lottie format. The .json format is directly supported by SkiaSharp.Skottie.Animation.

proposals.title: Extract JSON from .lottie ZIP
proposals.description: Use System.IO.Compression.ZipArchive to extract the animation JSON from the .lottie container, then pass it to Animation.Parse().

proposals.title: Add dotLottie format detection in Animation.Create
proposals.description: Detect ZIP file signature (PK header) in Animation.Create/TryCreate and automatically extract the JSON before passing to Skia. Would require adding System.IO.Compression dependency.

resolution.recommendedReason: Gives the reporter a working solution immediately while keeping the same .lottie files. The alternative (use .json) is simpler but requires re-downloading assets.

actionability.reason: The .lottie format is a ZIP container not supported by Skia's Skottie parser. Clear workarounds exist (use .json format or extract from ZIP). This is a format support gap, not a defect.

actions.description: Reclassify from bug to enhancement, remove Windows-only label (format support is cross-platform), add SkiaSharp area
Explain that .lottie is a ZIP container not supported by Skottie, provide extraction workaround
Cross-reference with #3242 (another Lottie animation format issue)
